lesson.title: Basics of Counting
lesson.explanation: Combinatorics counts structured arrangements. The product rule: if task A has m ways and task B has n ways, together they have m×n ways. Permutations count ordered arrangements: n!/(n-k)!. Combinations count unordered selections: C(n,k) = n!/[k!(n-k)!]. The pigeonhole principle: if n+1 items go into n boxes, at least one box has ≥2 items. Inclusion-exclusion counts unions: |A∪B| = |A| + |B| - |A∩B|.
